title: Make the audit trail tamper-evident
description: Protect audit records so that deletion or modification is detectable, not merely discouraged.
body: Use when designing or changing where audit evidence is stored. Do not store audit records in a table the application can UPDATE/DELETE freely. Actions: use append-only storage (WORM bucket, hash-chained records, or a separate-account log sink) so that any alteration breaks a verifiable chain or is blocked by storage policy, and restrict write/delete permissions away from the audited application's role. Validation signals include a test that attempting to modify or delete a record fails or is detected by chain verification. Risk boundary: a mutable audit store undermines every other control; treat lack of tamper-evidence as blocking and escalate before relying on the trail.
